findings.evidence: Signature at lines 60-65 has `[Finite ι]` and global `hcop`; the only use of coprimality in the body is `hcop i` at line 104.
findings.fix: State the helper pointwise, e.g. remove `[Finite ι]` and take only `hcop_i : ¬ (p : ℤ) ∣ d i` for the chosen `i`.
findings.issue: `legendre_eq_one_of_complete_split` proves a pointwise conclusion but assumes `[Finite ι]` and `hcop : ∀ i, ¬ (p : ℤ) ∣ d i`.

findings.evidence: Signature at lines 113-118 takes `hcop : ∀ i, ...` and `hqr : ∀ i, ...`; the body uses them only as `hcop i` and `hqr i` at lines 142 and 146.
findings.fix: Make this helper pointwise by taking `hcop_i : ¬ (p : ℤ) ∣ d i` and `hqr_i : legendreSym p (d i) = 1`, then let the wrapper supply those for each `i`.
findings.issue: `decompositionGroup_fixes_gen` also proves a pointwise fixed-generator result but assumes global `hcop` and `hqr` for every generator.

findings.evidence: `[Finite ι]` appears in this helper's signature at line 190, while the proof only iterates through `hfix : ∀ i` and `IntermediateField.adjoin_induction` over `Set.range r` at lines 201-214.
findings.fix: Drop `[Finite ι]` from this private wrapper; keep finiteness only on the public multiquadratic theorem where it is needed to construct the Galois instance.
findings.issue: `stabilizer_eq_bot_of_legendre` assumes `[Finite ι]`, but triviality from fixing all generators of `IntermediateField.adjoin ℚ (Set.range r)` does not require a finite index type.

summary: The refactor preserves the public theorem, but the new helper lemmas are stated with stronger whole-family and finiteness assumptions than their pointwise proofs need.
